title: Next Ariane 6 launch to carry 36 Amazon Leo satellites using upgraded boosters
summary: The next Ariane 6 launch will carry 36 Amazon Leo satellites using upgraded boosters. This marks Amazon's largest number of broadband satellites deployed to date.

title: Let’s build the moon base, but not lose sight of Mars
summary: NASA and the space industry are experiencing significant progress with the Artemis 2 mission, which flew a crew around the moon farther than any humans before. The focus remains on building a moon base while maintaining Mars as a long-term goal.

title: Qianfan constellation deployment hits 200 satellites with Long March 8 and 6A launches
summary: The Qianfan constellation, led by Shanghai, reached 200 satellites with recent Long March 8 and 6A launches. This follows two experimental flights and accelerates the constellation's deployment.

title: Satellite maker Apex’s valuation rises to $2.3 billion after latest $200 million raise
summary: Apex, a satellite manufacturer, raised $200 million in a funding round, increasing its valuation to $2.3 billion. The company plans to expand its in-house satellite production capabilities.

title: The Exploration Company completes drop test of Nyx capsule
summary: The Exploration Company successfully conducted a drop test of its Nyx spacecraft, designed for cargo transport to low Earth orbit. The test is a precursor to a planned 2028 orbital mission.

title: Axiom Space adds more than $175 million to funding round
summary: Axiom Space raised over $175 million in its latest funding round, with Japan’s largest bank joining as an investor.

title: HASC NDAA markup challenges Space Force on satellite programs
summary: The House Armed Services Committee (HASC) is challenging the U.S. Space Force by seeking to preserve a missile-warning satellite program that the Pentagon aims to cancel. It also criticizes recent tactical communications satellite procurement and GPS oversight.

title: AstroForge completes DeepSpace-2 spacecraft
summary: Asteroid mining startup AstroForge has finished building its DeepSpace-2 spacecraft, aiming for a launch later this year. The company incorporated lessons from a previous failed mission to improve the new spacecraft.

title: Delian Asparouhov and Philip Johnston on making the case for orbital data centers
summary: The article discusses a SpaceNews event where Jeff Foust interviews Delian Asparouhov of Founders Fund about the potential of orbital data centers. The conversation highlights reasons for increasing interest in this emerging space technology.

title: NASA working to streamline development of nuclear electric propulsion demo mission
summary: NASA is streamlining the development of a nuclear electric propulsion demonstration mission planned for launch in 2.5 years. The agency aims to accelerate the mission's timeline through improved management practices.

title: Meteor streaks across the sky above big observatory | Space photo of the day for June 5, 2026
summary: A meteor streaked across the night sky above Arizona's Kitt Peak National Observatory, creating a spectacular visual display. The event was featured as the Space Photo of the Day for June 5, 2026.

title: $1.77 trillion! SpaceX is about to become the 7th-most valuable American company
summary: SpaceX is set to become the 7th-most valuable American company with a valuation of $1.77 trillion following its IPO, where shares will be sold at $135 apiece.

title: One of the sky's rarest phenomena is back: How to see rare night-shining clouds this summer
summary: Night-shining noctilucent clouds, a rare atmospheric phenomenon, are visible before dawn and after sunset this summer. These clouds are the highest in Earth's atmosphere and can be seen under specific conditions.

title: Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS is blasting out a bunch of methane. Here's why that's weird
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope detected an unusual amount of methane around the interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS, highlighting its differences from solar system comets. This discovery challenges existing models of comet composition and behavior.

title: Glittering star cluster image reveals missing patch of stars: 'We were not looking for the gap, but we found it'
summary: The Euclid space telescope discovered an unexpected gap in the brightness distribution of red dwarf stars within the ancient globular cluster NGC 6397 while observing the star cluster. Researchers noted they were not specifically searching for this gap but stumbled upon it during their analysis.

title: August 2026 lunar eclipse: Everything you need to know about the 96% 'blood moon'
summary: A deep partial lunar eclipse, known as a 'blood moon,' will occur on August 27-28, 2026, and will be visible in North America. This event will be the best lunar eclipse visible on Earth until New Year's Eve 2028.

title: SpaceX launches back-to-back Starlink missions from both coasts 19 hours apart (photos)
summary: SpaceX successfully launched two consecutive Starlink missions from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California and Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida within 19 hours, deploying over 50 new satellites into orbit.

title: 12 of the weirdest 'Masters of the Universe' characters
summary: The article humorously lists 12 unusual characters from the 'Masters of the Universe' franchise, highlighting their bizarre designs and powers. It compares them to the strange patrons of the Mos Eisley Cantina from Star Wars.

title: NASA's dead Mars orbiter MAVEN will crash into the Red Planet in the next 100 years. It's not the only probe in the Mars morgue
summary: NASA's MAVEN Mars orbiter has been declared dead after 12 years of service and is expected to crash into Mars within the next 100 years.

title: Fighting Fire With Fire
summary: Prescribed burns are used in Australia’s Northern Territory to reduce the severity of wildfires during the season. This practice helps manage fire-prone ecosystems.

title: NASA Hosts 2026 Review on Advanced Composite Manufacturing
summary: NASA's Hi-Rate Composite Aircraft Manufacturing (HiCAM) project held a 2026 spring review at Langley Research Center with 150 consortium members. The meeting focused on progress updates from the 22-member public-private partnership.

title: NASA-Funded Study Shows Wildfire Smoke’s Hidden Ozone Toll
summary: A NASA-funded study reveals that wildfire smoke has significantly increased ground-level ozone pollution across much of the contiguous U.S. over the past decade, posing health risks even in areas far from active fires.

title: Colorful, Chaotic Jupiter
summary: NASA's Juno spacecraft captured a color-enhanced image of Jupiter's northern hemisphere during its 61st close flyby on May 12, 2024. Citizen scientist Gary Eason processed the raw data to enhance the image's color and clarity.

title: A Moonlit Earth as Seen From Artemis II
summary: An astronaut aboard Artemis II captured a striking photo of Earth from space, showcasing the planet's beauty and perspective from the Moon-bound mission. The image highlights humanity's connection to space and the significance of lunar exploration.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4908-4912: Goodbye Campo Marte, It’s Been Fun!
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover completed its 47th successful drill at 'Campo Marte' on Mars, marking the end of its operations at the site. The rover's team utilized the stationary period for additional scientific activities.

title: NASA Finds New Way Earth May Have Received Elements Needed for Life
summary: NASA-supported scientists discovered a new mechanism by which early Earth may have obtained essential elements for life, suggesting Jupiter played a key role in distributing these elements in the young solar system.

title: International Sea Level Satellite Observes El Niño Precursor
summary: The international Sentinel-6 Michael Freilich satellite observed a warm Kelvin wave precursor to El Niño, with higher, warmer water moving toward South America's west coast from March to May 2026. This data highlights early signs of the climate phenomenon before its full development.

title: Journey to the Center of the Virgo Cluster
summary: NASA/ESA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the active spiral galaxy Messier 88 (M88) as it journeys through the Virgo Cluster. Located 63 million light-years away, M88 is an active galaxy with a dynamic center.

title: NASA Drains 66-Million-Gallon Reservoir to Upgrade Critical Water System
summary: NASA drained a 66-million-gallon reservoir at Stennis Space Center to upgrade its High Pressure Industrial Water Facility, which is critical for rocket engine tests. The reservoir was pumped down to its lowest level since the 1960s.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This advancement marks a key milestone in the spacecraft's development.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: The Space Force is assessing damage caused by an anomaly and identifying future danger areas. Space Launch Delta 45 (SLD 45) has cleared the Eastern Range for operations.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for Artemis III have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key step in preparation for the mission, though timelines and landers remain uncertain.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has begun inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36, aiming for a return-to-flight (RTF) by the end of 2026.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week. Five missions are planned, with SpaceX and Chinese organizations leading the launches.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into Webb's 'Little Red Dots'. This discovery enhances understanding of early cosmic phenomena.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a catastrophic explosion during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36. The anomaly marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined the initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, utilizing rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative involves rebranding existing Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four dedicated to SpaceX's Starlink missions. Amazon's Kuiper constellation is also part of the launch manifest.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 hallmark achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant contributions by the European Space Agency.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical center in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers at ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section are testing materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space. Their work supports the success of ESA's missions.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This technology is opening new pathways for advancing space missions and outreach.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for a spacecraft's parachute system, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out.' The test aims to ensure the parachute's functionality under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling secure handling of objects in orbit. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: AstroForge Completes its DeepSpace-2 Spacecraft
summary: AstroForge has completed its DeepSpace-2 spacecraft, which aims to demonstrate rendezvous capabilities with an asteroid. This mission sets the foundation for future asteroid mining efforts planned before 2030.

title: Apex Raises $200M At $2.3B Valuation
summary: Apex raised $200 million in a funding round, valuing the company at $2.3 billion. This is the third time the company has raised $200 million in approximately 14 months.

title: US Commercial Space Stations Expand to Europe
summary: The US commercial space stations are expanding their reach to Europe as the International Space Station (ISS) nears the end of its operational life. This move ensures continued European access to Low Earth Orbit (LEO).

title: Recapping Planet’s Q1 With CFO and President Ashley Johnson
summary: Planet's Q1 performance was discussed by CFO and President Ashley Johnson, highlighting their differentiation from companies with binary outcomes. She emphasized their approach to revenue recognition and hardware delivery.

title: The Exploration Company Completes Nyx Drop Test
summary: The Exploration Company successfully completed a drop test for its Nyx capsule on May 19 to evaluate parachute performance and landing dynamics. The test used a dedicated drop-test vehicle to assess controlled descent and landing.

title: Austria’s First Commercial Sat is Ready for Launch
summary: Austria's first commercial satellite, Oasis Alpha, has been shipped by Vienna-based startup Tumbleweed to launch integrator Exolaunch for a planned July launch. The satellite is now ready for its upcoming mission.

title: Muon Space Unveils New, Larger Satellite Bus
summary: Muon Space has introduced a new, larger satellite bus named Condor-Ultra, which is three times the size of its predecessor Condor-XL and targets the emerging orbital data center market.

title: NASA Reverts to Original CLD Procurement Plan
summary: NASA has decided to return to its original Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLD) procurement plan, with industry feedback influencing the next steps. This move aims to advance lunar exploration missions under the original commercial strategy.

title: Blue Origin Commits to Return to Flight This Year
summary: Blue Origin has announced plans to return to flight operations by the end of the year after assessing damage to its launch pad and infrastructure. CEO Dave Limp confirmed the company's commitment in a post on X.

title: NDAA Would Cut SDA, RCO Offices
summary: The House's annual defense authorization bill proposes eliminating two Pentagon offices, the Space Development Agency (SDA) and the Rapid Capabilities Office (RCO), which are tasked with accelerating space acquisition. This move could significantly impact the Pentagon's efforts to modernize and streamline space programs.

title: Tototheo Global Taps Eutelsat for LEO Service for Maritime
summary: Tototheo Global has partnered with Eutelsat to provide Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite services for the maritime sector. This collaboration aims to enhance connectivity and communication solutions for maritime operations.

title: NGA Awards Planet $22M Luno B Option for Maritime Domain Awareness
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) awarded Planet Labs a $22 million contract option for AI-enabled maritime domain awareness data analytics. This builds on a previous contract to support U.S. Combatant Commands.

title: Infinite Orbits and Open Cosmos Plan ‘Tom & Jerry’ Space Rendezvous Mission
summary: Infinite Orbits has selected Open Cosmos to develop two satellites named 'Tom & Jerry' for a rendezvous and inspection mission in Low-Earth Orbit (LEO). The mission aims to demonstrate maneuvering and inspection capabilities.

title: Mbryonics and NTT to Collaborate on Optical Communications Infrastructure
summary: Mbryonics and NTT have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to collaborate on developing a high-speed optical communications infrastructure for space. The partnership aims to enhance space communications capabilities.

title: Muon Space Debuts High-Power ‘Ultra’ Satellite Designed for Orbital Data Centers
summary: Muon Space introduced a new high-power satellite platform called 'Ultra' designed for orbital data centers. The first mission is scheduled for delivery in 2028.

title: Beyond Gravity Releases New Propulsion Pointing Mechanism
summary: Beyond Gravity has introduced a new ultra-light propulsion pointing mechanism designed for high maneuverability in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) constellations. The two-axis mechanism aims to enhance the performance and efficiency of satellite operations.

title: Applied Aerospace & Defense IPOs to Raise $650M
summary: Applied Aerospace & Defense successfully launched an IPO on the New York Stock Exchange, raising $650 million by offering 32.5 million shares. The company, operating in the aerospace and defense sector, began trading on Wednesday.

title: Assessing SpaceX Finances, Addressable Market, and the AI Pitch Ahead of IPO
summary: SpaceX is preparing for its IPO next week, with analysts reviewing the company's financial filings to assess its finances and addressable market. The company's AI initiatives are also a key focus ahead of the public offering.

title: Impulse Space Raises Another Massive Funding Round in $500M Series D
summary: Impulse Space has raised $500 million in a Series D funding round led by 137 Ventures and Banner VC. The company is focused on advancing space transportation and in-space services.

title: Iceye U.S. Appoints ASRC Federal Exec Ann Stevens as CEO
summary: Iceye U.S. has appointed Ann Stevens as CEO of its subsidiary, replacing Eric Jensen who now serves as COO. Stevens joins from ASRC Federal.
